text: Each step I take in these dimly lit spaces fills me with a vague sense of unease and curiosity. The quiet, almost abandoned atmosphere makes me question the nature of presence and absence—how places normally buzzing with purpose can be rendered so still. Here, solitude presses in, and even the mundane fire hose on the wall or the R+D sign feels imbued with possibility, or perhaps, with a warning. Am I an intruder in this silence, or am I simply catching the world at rest?
